title: garrya laurifolia subsp. macrophylla
intro: Garrya laurifolia subsp. macrophylla, also known as the giant silktassel, is a plant species that belongs to the family Garryaceae and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Garrya laurifolia subsp. macrophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe gastrointestinal problems. It is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves can also be made into a decoction or infused in hot water to harness its medicinal properties.
